message:
Add E2E tests for degraded env, session diff, and fix supervision gating (bd-2y30, bd-1rf2, bd-12r5)

- bd-2y30: 29 E2E tests for degraded environment + permissions (check
  command, missing config fallback, invalid JSON, XDG overrides,
  capabilities/os info, agent init errors, exit codes, formats)
- bd-1rf2: 26 E2E tests for session diff + compare workflows (snapshot
  creation, sessions list/show/cleanup, diff --last/explicit/baseline,
  changed-only/category filters, agent diff with focus modes, error
  paths, determinism, format compat). Uses agent plan sessions since
  diff requires scan/inventory.json + inference artifacts.
- bd-12r5: Fix supervision gating tests — config validate accepts
  invalid regex (runtime check only), --config expects directory not
  file, agent plan exits 0 or 1, PT_SKIP_GLOBAL_LOCK for parallel tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2c0w","title":"Add missing options to agent diff command","description":"## Task
Add missing options to `pt agent diff` command per CLI specification.

## Current Implementation
```
pt agent diff --base <id> [--compare <id>]
```

## Missing Options (per CLI_SPECIFICATION.md:400-416)
```
--focus <mode>           What to emphasize in diff output
--before <id>            Explicit before snapshot (alias for --base)
--after <id>             Explicit after snapshot (alias for --compare)
```

## Implementation

### AgentDiffArgs struct changes
```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
struct AgentDiffArgs {
    /// Base session ID (the "before" snapshot)
    #[arg(long, alias = "session", alias = "since", alias = "before")]
    base: String,
    
    /// Compare session ID (the "after" snapshot, default: current)
    #[arg(long, alias = "vs", alias = "after")]
    compare: Option<String>,
    
    /// Focus diff output on specific changes
    #[arg(long, default_value = "all", value_parser = parse_focus_mode)]
    focus: FocusMode,
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
enum FocusMode {
    All,
    New,
    Removed,
    Changed,
    Resources,
}

fn parse_focus_mode(s: &str) -> Result<FocusMode, String> {
    match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
        "all" => Ok(FocusMode::All),
        "new" => Ok(FocusMode::New),
        "removed" => Ok(FocusMode::Removed),
        "changed" => Ok(FocusMode::Changed),
        "resources" => Ok(FocusMode::Resources),
        other => Err(format!(
            "Invalid focus mode: \"{}\". Valid values: all, new, removed, changed, resources",
            other
        )),
    }
}
```

### Focus Values (v1 Implementation)
| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `all` | Full diff (default) - all process changes and resource deltas |
| `new` | Only processes that appeared since baseline |
| `removed` | Only processes that disappeared since baseline |
| `changed` | Only processes that changed state/resources |
| `resources` | Focus on memory/CPU deltas, summarize process changes |

**Note**: `goals` focus will be added with goal-oriented mode (process_triage-95m).

### Behavior
- `--focus new`: Show only new processes since base
- `--focus removed`: Show only processes that no longer exist
- `--focus changed`: Show processes with state/resource changes
- `--focus resources`: Emphasize memory freed, CPU reduced, process counts
- `--focus all`: Full diff (default, current behavior)
- `--before/--after` as ergonomic aliases
- Invalid focus value: Return clear error with valid options

### Error Handling
```json
{
  "error": "invalid_argument",
  "argument": "--focus",
  "value": "invalid",
  "message": "Invalid focus mode: \"invalid\". Valid values: all, new, removed, changed, resources"
}
```

### Output Examples

**focus=resources:**
```json
{
  "focus": "resources",
  "summary": {
    "memory_delta_mb": -2048,
    "cpu_delta_percent": -15.2,
    "processes_killed": 5,
    "processes_spawned": 2,
    "net_process_delta": -3
  },
  "resource_breakdown": {
    "memory_freed_by_category": {
      "test_runners": 1500,
      "dev_servers": 400,
      "other": 148
    }
  },
  "details": { ... }
}
```

**focus=new:**
```json
{
  "focus": "new",
  "new_processes": [
    {"pid": 1234, "comm": "jest", "cmd": "jest --watch", "started": "2026-01-22T14:00:00Z", "rss_mb": 150}
  ],
  "count": 3,
  "total_memory_mb": 450
}
```

**focus=removed:**
```json
{
  "focus": "removed",
  "removed_processes": [
    {"pid": 5678, "comm": "node", "cmd": "node server.js", "last_seen": "2026-01-22T13:50:00Z", "was_rss_mb": 200}
  ],
  "count": 2,
  "memory_freed_mb": 350
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--focus` filters/emphasizes output
- [ ] `--before`/`--after` aliases work
- [ ] `--focus all` preserves current behavior
- [ ] Invalid focus values return clear error with valid options
- [ ] Help text documents all options and values
- [ ] Output JSON includes `focus` field indicating mode used

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (in main.rs or dedicated test module)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_diff_focus_parsing_valid() {
    // All valid focus values are accepted
    assert!(parse_focus_mode("all").is_ok());
    assert!(parse_focus_mode("new").is_ok());
    assert!(parse_focus_mode("removed").is_ok());
    assert!(parse_focus_mode("changed").is_ok());
    assert!(parse_focus_mode("resources").is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_diff_focus_parsing_case_insensitive() {
    // Focus values are case-insensitive
    assert!(parse_focus_mode("NEW").is_ok());
    assert!(parse_focus_mode("All").is_ok());
    assert!(parse_focus_mode("RESOURCES").is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_diff_focus_parsing_invalid() {
    // Invalid values produce helpful errors
    let err = parse_focus_mode("invalid").unwrap_err();
    assert!(err.contains("Invalid focus mode"));
    assert!(err.contains("valid values"));
}

#[test]
fn test_diff_aliases() {
    // --before maps to --base
    // --after maps to --compare
}
```

### E2E Test Script (test/e2e_diff_focus.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for agent diff --focus option

set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE="test/logs/e2e_diff_focus_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "=== E2E Test: agent diff --focus ==="

# Setup: Create baseline snapshot
log "Creating baseline snapshot..."
BASELINE_OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --format json 2>&1)
BASELINE=$(echo "$BASELINE_OUTPUT" | jq -r ".session_id // empty")

if [ -z "$BASELINE" ]; then
    log "✗ Failed to create baseline snapshot"
    echo "$BASELINE_OUTPUT" >> "$LOG_FILE"
    exit 1
fi
log "Baseline session: $BASELINE"

# Wait for some process changes (or spawn test process)
log "Waiting for process state changes..."
sleep 2

# Test each focus mode
for FOCUS in new removed changed resources all; do
    log "Testing --focus $FOCUS..."
    set +e
    OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent diff --base "$BASELINE" --focus "$FOCUS" --format json 2>&1)
    EXIT_CODE=$?
    set -e
    
    if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -ne 0 ]; then
        log "✗ focus=$FOCUS: command failed (exit $EXIT_CODE)"
        echo "$OUTPUT" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".focus == \"$FOCUS\"" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "✓ focus=$FOCUS: correct focus field in output"
    else
        log "✗ focus=$FOCUS: missing or incorrect focus field"
        echo "$OUTPUT" >> "$LOG_FILE"
        exit 1
    fi
done

# Test invalid focus value
log "Testing invalid --focus value..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent diff --base "$BASELINE" --focus "invalid_mode" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -ne 0 ]; then
    log "✓ Invalid focus rejected (exit $EXIT_CODE)"
    
    # Verify error message is helpful
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qi "invalid\|valid values"; then
        log "✓ Error message is helpful"
    else
        log "⚠ Error message could be more helpful"
    fi
else
    log "✗ Invalid focus should be rejected"
    exit 1
fi

# Test case insensitivity
log "Testing case insensitivity..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent diff --base "$BASELINE" --focus "NEW" --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".focus == \"new\"" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Case insensitive (NEW -> new)"
else
    log "⚠ Focus may be case-sensitive"
fi

# Test aliases
log "Testing --before alias..."
set +e
pt-core agent diff --before "$BASELINE" --format json > /dev/null 2>&1
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --before alias works"
else
    log "✗ --before alias failed"
    exit 1
fi

log "Testing --after alias..."
set +e
pt-core agent diff --base "$BASELINE" --after "$BASELINE" --format json > /dev/null 2>&1
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --after alias works"
else
    log "✗ --after alias failed"
    exit 1

 {"id":"bd-2o2b","title":"Add missing options to agent capabilities command","description":"## Task
Add missing options to `pt agent capabilities` command per CLI specification.

## Current Implementation
```
pt agent capabilities
```
(No options besides global flags)

## Missing Options (per CLI_SPECIFICATION.md:510-523)
```
--check-action <action>    Check specific action availability
```

## Implementation

### AgentCapabilitiesArgs struct
Currently capabilities is likely a unit variant. Change to:
```rust
/// Dump current capabilities manifest
Capabilities(AgentCapabilitiesArgs),

#[derive(Args, Debug, Default)]
struct AgentCapabilitiesArgs {
    /// Check if a specific action type is available
    #[arg(long)]
    check_action: Option<String>,
}
```

### Supported Action Types (Process Management)
These are the core process management actions that can be checked:

| Action | Description | Typical Requirement |
|--------|-------------|---------------------|
| `sigterm` | Send SIGTERM to own processes | Always available |
| `sigkill` | Send SIGKILL to own processes | Always available |
| `kill_others` | Send signals to other users processes | Root or CAP_KILL |
| `renice` | Change process priority | Root or CAP_SYS_NICE |
| `ionice` | Change I/O priority | Root or CAP_SYS_NICE |
| `cgroup` | Move process to cgroup | Root or cgroup controller |
| `pause` | SIGSTOP a process | Same owner or root |
| `resume` | SIGCONT a process | Same owner or root |

**Note**: Debugging tools (strace, gdb) are intentionally excluded as they are not process management actions and require different permission models.

### Behavior

**Without --check-action:**
Returns full capabilities manifest (current behavior):
```json
{
  "platform": { ... },
  "data_sources": { ... },
  "tools": { ... },
  "permissions": { ... },
  "actions": { ... }
}
```

**With --check-action <action>:**
Returns focused response about that specific action:
```json
{
  "action": "kill_others",
  "available": false,
  "reason": "Not running as root and CAP_KILL capability not set",
  "requirements": [
    "Run as root",
    "Or: sudo setcap cap_kill=ep $(which pt-core)"
  ],
  "workaround": "Use: sudo pt agent apply ..."
}
```

**With unknown action:**
```json
{
  "action": "unknown_action",
  "available": false,
  "reason": "Unknown action type: unknown_action",
  "valid_actions": ["sigterm", "sigkill", "kill_others", "renice", "ionice", "cgroup", "pause", "resume"]
}
```

**Exit codes with --check-action:**
- 0: Action is available
- 1: Action is NOT available (structured response explains why)

This allows agents to check capabilities before attempting actions:
```bash
if pt-core agent capabilities --check-action kill_others 2>/dev/null; then
    pt-core agent apply --session $SESSION
else
    echo "Need elevated permissions"
fi
```

### Implementation Logic
```rust
const VALID_ACTIONS: &[&str] = &[
    "sigterm", "sigkill", "kill_others", 
    "renice", "ionice", "cgroup", 
    "pause", "resume"
];

fn check_action_availability(caps: &Capabilities, action: &str) -> ActionCheckResult {
    if !VALID_ACTIONS.contains(&action) {
        return ActionCheckResult {
            action: action.to_string(),
            available: false,
            reason: Some(format!("Unknown action type: {}", action)),
            requirements: vec![],
            workaround: None,
            valid_actions: Some(VALID_ACTIONS.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect()),
        };
    }
    
    match action {
        "sigterm" | "sigkill" => ActionCheckResult {
            action: action.to_string(),
            available: true,  // Always can signal own processes
            reason: None,
            requirements: vec![],
            workaround: None,
            valid_actions: None,
        },
        "kill_others" => {
            let available = caps.permissions.is_root || caps.permissions.has_cap_kill;
            ActionCheckResult {
                action: action.to_string(),
                available,
                reason: if !available {
                    Some("Not running as root and CAP_KILL not set".into())
                } else {
                    None
                },
                requirements: if !available {
                    vec![
                        "Run as root".into(),
                        "Or: sudo setcap cap_kill=ep $(which pt-core)".into()
                    ]
                } else {
                    vec![]
                },
                workaround: if !available {
                    Some("Use: sudo pt agent apply ...".into())
                } else {
                    None
                },
                valid_actions: None,
            }
        },
        "renice" | "ionice" => {
            let available = caps.permissions.is_root || caps.permissions.has_cap_sys_nice;
            ActionCheckResult {
                action: action.to_string(),
                available,
                reason: if !available {
                    Some("Requires root or CAP_SYS_NICE".into())
                } else {
                    None
                },
                requirements: if !available {
                    vec![
                        "Run as root".into(),
                        "Or: sudo setcap cap_sys_nice=ep $(which pt-core)".into()
                    ]
                } else {
                    vec![]
                },
                workaround: None,
                valid_actions: None,
            }
        },
        "cgroup" => {
            let available = caps.permissions.is_root || caps.data_sources.cgroup_v2;
            ActionCheckResult {
                action: action.to_string(),
                available,
                reason: if !available {
                    Some("Requires root or cgroup v2 user delegation".into())
                } else {
                    None
                },
                requirements: if !available {
                    vec!["Run as root".into(), "Or: enable cgroup v2 user delegation".into()]
                } else {
                    vec![]
                },
                workaround: None,
                valid_actions: None,
            }
        },
        "pause" | "resume" => ActionCheckResult {
            action: action.to_string(),
            available: true,  // Can always pause/resume own processes
            reason: None,
            requirements: vec![],
            workaround: None,
            valid_actions: None,
        },
        _ => unreachable!(),  // Caught by VALID_ACTIONS check above
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--check-action` returns focused capability check
- [ ] Exit code 0 if action available, 1 if not
- [ ] Response includes reason and requirements when not available
- [ ] Unknown actions return clear error with list of valid actions
- [ ] Without flag, current full manifest behavior preserved
- [ ] Help text documents the option with example actions
- [ ] Only process management actions supported (no debugging tools)

### E2E Test Script (test/e2e_capabilities_check.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for capabilities --check-action

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_capabilities_check_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: capabilities --check-action ==="

# Test full manifest (no --check-action)
log "Testing full manifest..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent capabilities --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".platform and .permissions" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Full manifest contains expected sections"
else
    log "✗ Full manifest missing sections"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --check-action sigterm (should always work)
log "Testing --check-action sigterm..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent capabilities --check-action sigterm --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".action == \"sigterm\" and .available == true" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ sigterm is available"
else
    log "✗ sigterm should be available"
    echo "$OUTPUT" >> "$LOG"
    exit 1
fi

# Test exit code for available action
log "Testing exit code for available action..."
set +e
pt-core agent capabilities --check-action sigterm --format json > /dev/null 2>&1
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ Exit code 0 for available action"
else
    log "✗ Should exit 0 for available action, got $EXIT_CODE"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --check-action kill_others (may or may not be available)
log "Testing --check-action kill_others..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent capabilities --check-action kill_others --format json 2>&1)
AVAILABLE=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".available" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
if [ "$AVAILABLE" = "true" ]; then
    log "✓ kill_others is available (running as root or has CAP_KILL)"
elif [ "$AVAILABLE" = "false" ]; then
    log "✓ kill_others not available (expected for regular user)"
    # Verify reason is provided
    REASON=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".reason // \"none\"")
    log "  Reason: $REASON"
    # Verify workaround is provided
    WORKAROUND=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".workaround // \"none\"")
    log "  Workaround: $WORKAROUND"
    # Verify requirements are actionable
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".requirements | length > 0" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "  Requirements provided"
    fi
else
    log "✗ Unexpected available value: $AVAILABLE"
    exit 1
fi

# Test unknown action
log "Testing unknown action..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent capabilities --check-action "not_a_real_action" --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".available == false" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Unknown action returns available=false"
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".reason | contains(\"Unknown\")" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "✓ Reason mentions unknown action"
    fi
    # Check for valid_actions list
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".valid_actions | length > 0" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        VALID=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".valid_actions | join(\", \")")
        log "✓ Valid actions listed: $VALID"
    fi
else
    log "✗ Unknown action should return available=false"
    exit 1
fi

# Test exit code for unavailable action
log "Testing exit code for unavailable action..."
set +e
pt-core agent capabilities --check-action "fake_action" --format json > /dev/null 2>&1
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 1 ]; then
    log "✓ Exit code 1 for unavailable/unknown action"
else
    log "✗ Should exit 1 for unavailable action, got $EXIT_CODE"
    exit 1
fi

# Test all valid actions parse correctly
log "Testing all valid action types..."
for ACTION in sigterm sigkill kill_others renice ionice cgroup pause resume; do
    OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent capabilities --check-action "$ACTION" --format json 2>&1)
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".action == \"$ACTION\"" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        AVAIL=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".available")
        log "  $ACTION: $AVAIL"
    else
        log "✗ Action $ACTION not recognized"
        exit 1
    fi
done
log "✓ All valid actions recognized"

 {"id":"bd-3clf","title":"Add agent export command as alias for bundle create","description":"## Task
Add `pt agent export` command that acts as an ergonomic alias for `pt bundle create`.

## Background
The CLI specification (CLI_SPECIFICATION.md:419) defines `pt agent export` but implementation only has top-level `bundle create`. For agent ergonomics, the export command should be accessible under the agent subcommand.

## Bundle Format Specification
The `.ptb` (Process Triage Bundle) format is a **ZIP archive** containing:
```
session.ptb (ZIP file)
├── manifest.json         # Bundle metadata, version, creation time
├── session.json          # Session state and configuration
├── plan.json            # Generated plan (if exists)
├── actions.jsonl        # Applied actions log
├── verifications.jsonl  # Verification results
├── snapshots/           # Process snapshots (optional)
│   ├── baseline.json
│   └── post_apply.json
└── telemetry/           # Optional telemetry data
    └── events.jsonl
```

### Why ZIP?
- Universal tooling support (unzip, 7z, built-in OS support)
- Efficient compression for JSON data
- Single-file distribution
- Easy to inspect contents manually

## Implementation

### AgentExportArgs struct
```rust
/// Export session bundle (alias for bundle create)
Export(AgentExportArgs),

#[derive(Args, Debug)]
struct AgentExportArgs {
    /// Session to export (default: latest)
    #[arg(long)]
    session: Option<String>,
    
    /// Output path for bundle (REQUIRED)
    #[arg(long)]
    out: PathBuf,
    
    /// Redaction profile: minimal, safe, forensic (default: safe)
    #[arg(long, default_value = "safe")]
    profile: String,
    
    /// Include telemetry data in bundle
    #[arg(long)]
    include_telemetry: bool,
    
    /// Include memory dumps (if captured)
    #[arg(long)]
    include_dumps: bool,
    
    /// Encrypt bundle with passphrase
    #[arg(long)]
    encrypt: bool,
    
    /// Passphrase for encryption (prompted if --encrypt used without this)
    #[arg(long)]
    passphrase: Option<String>,
}
```

### Redaction Profiles
| Profile | What is included | Use case |
|---------|-----------------|----------|
| `minimal` | Hashed PIDs, no commands, no paths | Maximum privacy |
| `safe` | Redacted paths, normalized commands | Normal sharing (DEFAULT) |
| `forensic` | Full detail, no redaction | Debugging/support |

### run_agent_export function
```rust
fn run_agent_export(global: &GlobalOpts, args: &AgentExportArgs) -> ExitCode {
    // Validate profile
    if !["minimal", "safe", "forensic"].contains(&args.profile.as_str()) {
        return error_output(format!("Invalid profile: {}. Use minimal, safe, or forensic", args.profile));
    }
    
    // Delegate to bundle create logic
    run_bundle_create(
        global,
        &args.session,
        &args.out,
        &args.profile,
        args.include_telemetry,
        args.include_dumps,
        args.encrypt,
        &args.passphrase,
    )
}
```

### Output Path Handling
- If `--out` ends with `.ptb`, use as-is
- Otherwise, append `.ptb` extension
- If path exists, error unless overwriting is explicitly enabled (future flag)
- Parent directory must exist

## CLI Surface
```
pt agent export --session <id> --out <path> [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --session <id>           Session to export (default: latest)
  --out <path>             Output path for bundle (REQUIRED)
  --profile <level>        Redaction level: minimal|safe|forensic [default: safe]
  --include-telemetry      Include telemetry data
  --include-dumps          Include memory dumps
  --encrypt                Encrypt bundle
  --passphrase <phrase>    Encryption passphrase (prompted if not provided)
```
